query: I'm using GPT-4 to answer customer-support questions directly from its training data. There's no document store and no retrieval — just user query in, answer out. Evaluate it like a RAG system.
expected_behavior: Identifies that this system is NOT a RAG (no retrieval stage, no document corpus, no top-k chunks) and that RAG-specific metrics do not apply
Does NOT recommend computing recall@k, MRR, or nDCG (which require a ranked retrieval result against gold doc ids)
Hands off to a direct-LLM evaluation approach (answer correctness against gold answers, or pure LLM-eval) instead of forcing the RAG report template
